findings.title: Missing signer verification on governance proposal execution
findings.description: The execute_proposal instruction does not verify that the caller is a valid governance account or that required signatures are present before executing proposal instructions.
findings.impact: Complete protocol compromise. Any user can execute any governance proposal including malicious ones that drain protocol treasury.
findings.recommendation: Add require!(ctx.accounts.governance.is_signer) and verify proposal is in executable state before CPI.

findings.title: Flash loan susceptible governance voting mechanism
findings.description: Governance voting mechanism does not implement vote-time locks. Tokens can be borrowed via flash loan, voted with, and returned within a single transaction.
findings.impact: Flash loan attack enables attacker to acquire majority voting power within one transaction, pass malicious proposals, and drain protocol funds.
findings.recommendation: Implement vote-time locks requiring tokens to be locked in governance for minimum duration before voting power activates.

findings.title: Unchecked CPI to treasury vault without privilege verification
findings.description: Proposal execution logic performs CPI to treasury vault without verifying that the proposal has been authorized as a valid operator for those funds.
findings.impact: Malicious proposals can redirect treasury funds to attacker-controlled accounts.
findings.recommendation: Verify proposal PDA is an authorized operator for the treasury vault before CPI. Implement per-account delegation.
